summary: Frontend-only E2E testing of Colorfly Studio palette generator. 14/15 required feature flows pass. One CRITICAL bug found: localStorage persistence of saved palettes is broken after page reload (data wiped to []). Also identified a secondary UX issue with framer-motion layout animations when changing palette size (grid visually broken for ~1-3s on size change). All data-testid selectors are present and functional.

ui_bugs.issue: CRITICAL: Saved palettes do NOT persist across page reload. localStorage key 'colorfly:savedPalettes' is overwritten with '[]' on mount. Root cause: the persist useEffect (line 43-49) fires on mount with initial empty state `saved=[]` and overwrites localStorage BEFORE the load useEffect's setState propagates. Exacerbated by React StrictMode double-invoking effects. After reload the Guardadas counter resets to (0) and drawer is empty.

When changing palette size (e.g. 6 -> 9) the grid visually shows only partial columns clustered at the right edge for ~1-3 seconds while framer-motion layout animations settle. The DOM contains correct number of cards with correct widths (verified via JS), but visually the layout flashes broken. During the transient animation the DOM can temporarily contain duplicate cards (e.g. 17 nodes observed before settling to 9). This is because AnimatePresence with mode='popLayout' plus `layout` prop plus 0.45s+index*0.05s stagger produces a long perceivable settle time.

issues: Radio inputs are sr-only (visually hidden); selection is driven only by label backgrounds. Keyboard users can still Tab to them and arrow-key between, but the visible focus ring from the label is minimal. Consider adding :focus-within outline on the <label> or <fieldset>.
Skip link 'Saltar al contenido' appears correctly on Tab (verified) — good a11y.

action_items: HIGH/BLOCKER: Fix persistence. Replace the separate load + persist useEffect pair with lazy initial state OR guard persist effect with a `hasLoaded` ref. Recommended fix: `const [saved, setSaved] = useState(() => { try { const raw = localStorage.getItem('colorfly:savedPalettes'); return raw ? JSON.parse(raw) : []; } catch { return []; } });` then remove the load useEffect entirely. Keep the persist useEffect (it will now only write after real state changes).

MEDIUM: Reduce framer-motion layout settle time on size change. Options: (a) remove `layout` prop from ColorCard (positions are already flex-driven), (b) shorten transition duration from 0.45s to ~0.2s, (c) remove or cap the `delay: index * 0.05` stagger, or (d) add a fixed `min-width` on each card so the exit animation doesn't collapse columns.
critical_code_review_comments: PaletteGenerator.jsx:33-49 — Load + Persist useEffect pattern is racy. Initial persist effect writes `[]` over real localStorage data on every mount. Use lazy useState initializer instead.

ColorGrid.jsx:10-19 — `layout` prop on motion.div combined with AnimatePresence popLayout and long stagger (delay: index*0.05, duration 0.45) produces visible broken-grid flash on size changes. Consider reducing or removing.
PaletteGenerator.jsx:117-129 — Spacebar listener correctly excludes input/textarea/contentEditable. Good.

colorUtils.js:48 — id generator uses `Date.now()-randomSlice`. In rapid succession (e.g. inside generatePalette for-loop) multiple colors can share identical Date.now(). Fine since Math.random tail differentiates, but consider crypto.randomUUID() for robustness.
SavedPalettesDrawer.jsx:20-27 — Hidden SheetTrigger is unused (the real trigger is the external `open-saved-palettes` button that sets state directly). Can be removed to simplify.
No backend present — 100% frontend MVP as expected, localStorage only.

success_rate.frontend: 93% (14/15 core flows pass; 1 CRITICAL persistence bug)

context_for_next_testing_agent: All data-testid are present: generate-palette-button, size-selector-{6,8,9}, format-toggle-{HEX,HSL}, color-card-{i}, color-code-{i}, copy-color-button-{i}, lock-color-button-{i}, save-palette-button, open-saved-palettes, saved-palettes-drawer, restore-palette-{id}, delete-palette-{id}. Verified working: generate, spacebar, size change (visible card count correct after animations settle ~2s), HEX/HSL toggle, clipboard copy + toast, lock persistence across regenerate, save/restore/delete inside drawer, semantic header/main/footer, aria-labels on all interactive buttons, visible focus outline on Tab. BROKEN: localStorage persistence across reload (data wiped). When retesting after fix, verify: (1) save 2 palettes, (2) reload, (3) open drawer, (4) both palettes still listed with same hex values, (5) localStorage content matches before/after reload. Also revalidate size-change animation smoothness.
rca of the issue: Persistence bug RCA: React runs useEffects in source order after commit. Effect #1 (load) reads localStorage and calls setSaved(realData) — this only schedules a state update. Effect #2 (persist) then runs in the SAME pass with the stale closure value saved=[] and writes JSON.stringify([]) = '[]' to localStorage, overwriting the real data. Although the state update from Effect #1 triggers a re-render that re-fires Effect #2 with the new saved value, React StrictMode (enabled in index.js) double-invokes effects on mount: the first cycle's state update gets unmounted, then on the second mount, Effect #1 reads the now-'[]' localStorage, finds empty array, no state change, and Effect #2 writes '[]' again. Final state: localStorage = '[]', saved = []. Reproduction: (1) open app, (2) click Guardar, (3) reload — Guardadas counter shows (0). Mitigation: use lazy useState initializer so saved state is populated synchronously from localStorage during the initial render, eliminating the race entirely.
